disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A tight cluster of small fluid-filled blisters on a red base erupted overnight on the right cheek, with some already burst leaving shallow raw spots and early golden crusting.
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe exactly what the spots look like?
How did the blisters come up and what are they doing now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's a little cluster of tiny blisters on a red patch; a few have popped and gone raw with a golden crust starting. They came up overnight.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A 48-hour prodrome of localised tingling and burning at the same site preceded the blisters (a neuropathic prodrome).
trigger_question_examples: Did you feel anything at that spot before the blisters appeared?
Was there any tingling, burning or itching beforehand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — for a day or two before, that patch tingled and burned before any blisters showed up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The eruption is on the cheek immediately below the eye, close to the lower eyelid margin and appearing to spread toward the eye.
trigger_question_examples: Whereabouts on your face is it, exactly?
How close is it to your eye?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's on my right cheek just under my eye, near my lower eyelid, and it feels like it's creeping toward my eye.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A recent period of intense stress and ultraviolet (sun/beach) exposure in the days before onset, plus a past history of occasional cold sores — recognised triggers/precedents for a herpes simplex reactivation.
trigger_question_examples: Have you had cold sores before, and was there any stress or sun exposure recently?
Anything that tends to trigger these for you?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I get the odd cold sore on my lip. I've been really stressed lately and I was out in the sun at the beach a few days before this started.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No current ocular symptoms — the eye is not red, there is no eye pain, no light sensitivity, no gritty/foreign-body sensation and no blurring or change in vision — screening (by patient report only) for herpes simplex keratitis / ocular involvement.
trigger_question_examples: Is your eye red or painful, and can you see normally out of it?
Any sensitivity to light, watering, or a gritty feeling in the eye?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — my eye isn't red or painful, lights don't bother me, it's not gritty and my vision seems normal to me. It's the skin on my cheek that's the problem.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The pain is a mild throb rather than deep severe nerve pain, the eruption is confined to the cheek with no forehead, scalp, upper-eyelid or tip-of-nose lesions — arguing against herpes zoster ophthalmicus (V1 shingles).
trigger_question_examples: Is the pain a deep, severe, burning nerve pain, or more of a mild soreness?
Do you have any blisters on your forehead, scalp, the side of your nose or your upper eyelid?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's only a mild throb, not a deep nerve pain, and there's nothing on my forehead, scalp, upper eyelid or the tip of my nose — just the one patch on my cheek.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no facial or eyelid swelling and no worsening spreading redness — no features of secondary bacterial spread or periorbital/orbital cellulitis; the patient is systemically well but highly anxious and lives alone.
trigger_question_examples: Do you have a fever, or is your eyelid or face swelling up?
Is the redness spreading quickly or is your eye being pushed forward or hard to move?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, my eyelid and face aren't swollen, and the redness isn't spreading out beyond the blisters. I just feel anxious, especially living on my own.
